articles.title: Punjab records highest GST collections in April: FM Harpal Singh Cheema
articles.description: GST collections: Punjab records highest-ever April revenue with Rs 2,987 crore Finance Minister Harpal Singh Cheema says strong enforcement and compliance drove surge, while adjusted growth shows steady gains despite IGST factor and national trends.

articles.title: Om Birla rejigs 4 House panels for 2026-27; most chiefs stay on
articles.description: Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla has reconstituted four key financial and department-related parliamentary committees. These committees will serve for the 2026-27 financial year. Several prominent leaders have retained their chairpersonships. Faggan Singh Kulaste heads the Committee on the Welfare of Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes. Baijayant Jay Panda chairs the Committee on Public Undertakings.

articles.title: 19 years on, demand for direct train from Vizag to Bengaluru remains unfulfilled
articles.description: Despite rising demand, a direct train from Vizag to Bengaluru remains unfulfilled, hindering economic growth between the two IT hubs.

articles.title: Chief Minister Lalduhoma attends Mizoram Rural Bank Branch Managers’ Conclave 2026
articles.description: Mizoram Rural Bank is recognized as one of India's most progressive Regional Rural Banks. The bank achieved a remarkable Credit-Deposit Ratio of 63.28 percent. Its total business turnover reached over 10,600 crore rupees. Mizoram Rural Bank also leads in digital transactions in Northeast India. The Chief Minister lauded its contribution to state development.

articles.title: Plurilaterals without guardrails can fragment trading system: Experts
articles.description: Global trade volumes are growing despite concerns about the WTO. Experts discussed reforming the WTO's rules and governance. They highlighted the rise of 'precautionism' and the need for balanced reform. India's role in plurilateral negotiations was debated. The WTO remains indispensable for global trade and multilateralism.

articles.title: J&K PMGSY Funds: Udhampur-Kathua-Doda gets massive 43% share
articles.description: Jitendra Singh's Udhampur-Kathua-Doda constituency secures a staggering ₹1,524.46 crore, nearly 43% of the total ₹3,566 crore PMGSY-IV allocation for Jammu and Kashmir, aimed at boosting rural infrastructure and last-mile connectivity.

articles.title: Palamu District Gears Up for House Listing and Census: May 16 - June 14
articles.description: Palamu district is set to conduct its house listing and census from May 16 to June 14, involving 3,816 enumerators and 722 supervisors. Each enumerator will cover approximately 150-180 houses or 800 individuals. Officials emphasize that enumerators will not ask for personal data, urging households to remain vigilant.

articles.description: Life Insurance Corporation of India (LIC) will however remain under a separate framework, with foreign investment capped at 20% under the automatic route.

articles.description: Despite the IPO's horrific first-day performance, Ackman is confident that "it's going to be a very good long-term ride" for his new shareholders.

articles.title: Aries Horoscope Today, May 3, 2026: Don't combine emotions with spending
articles.description: Exciting financial gains are just around the corner with pending payments on the verge of resolution. While it's natural to feel antsy, remember that gradual improvement trumps the quest for flawless immediacy. In personal connections, take a moment to breathe before responding rashly. Dedication is key in your professional and educational pursuits.

articles.title: Cabinet clears GTTC centre for Jewargi; ₹73.75-crore project to boost skill training
articles.description: State government approves ₹73.75 crore GTTC in Jewargi to enhance skill training and boost local employment opportunities.

articles.title: Govt ensures uninterrupted fuel supply amid West Asia crisis: Ministry
articles.description: The government has assured uninterrupted supply of LPG, PNG, and CNG despite the West Asia crisis. It urged citizens to avoid panic buying and cut excise duty on petrol and diesel to shield consumers from high global crude prices.

articles.title: Kerala man shuts Rs 3 lakh monthly revenue startup, takes Rs 25,000 sales job. Here’s why
articles.description: A Kerala man watched his startup dream collapse and chose a steady salary over another risky venture. That one difficult decision later opened doors to a global business and a real-world MBA.

articles.title: AI can't steal your job! A China twist can avert AI jobs apocalypse
articles.description: A Hangzhou court has set a precedent, ruling that companies cannot use AI automation as a blanket justification for firing workers. The decision emphasizes contract law and employer responsibility, requiring genuine impossibility of role performance and fair reassignment. This ruling shifts focus from AI's inevitability to accountability for job displacement costs.

articles.description: South Central Railways (SCR) requests 1,125 acres in Telangana for four mega coaching terminals

articles.title: ‘We’re not like India’: Pakistan minister admits to no strategic oil reserves, says stocks for only '5-7 days’
articles.description: He went on to reveal that Islamabad has crude stocks for just “five to seven days”, in contrast, India can tap into its reserves “with just a single signature.”

articles.title: Microcap stocks steal show in April, outpace, Nifty, smallcap, midcaps, largecaps. What’s driving the surge?
articles.description: Indian equities surged in April 2026, led by strong gains in mid, small, and microcaps despite persistent macro risks, foreign outflows, and currency weakness. Market performance diverged from fundamentals, which remained stable, suggesting price-driven momentum rather than earnings-led improvement across segments.

articles.title: Indian ETF Inflows Hit Record Rs 1.81 Lakh Crore in FY26, Gold and Silver Dominate
articles.description: Equity ETFs saw inflows of Rs 77,780 crore in FY26, contributing about 43 per cent, while debt ETFs remained a marginal segment with Rs 4,066 crore.

articles.title: Gold investment boom: Why Indians are choosing bullion over jewellery now
articles.description: India’s gold demand is increasingly being driven by investors rather than jewellery buyers, marking a clear shift in consumption patterns. Rising prices, ETF inflows, and global uncertainties are accelerating gold’s transition from ornament to core portfolio asset.

articles.title: RBI Governor Malhotra calls for deeper, more efficient financial markets as India shows resilience amid global headwinds
articles.description: Indian financial markets are showing strength amid global challenges. RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra highlighted that future growth hinges on increasing liquidity and participation. The economy is growing fast, with inflation under control. The banking sector is robust. The RBI is implementing measures to improve market efficiency and transparency.

articles.title: India's resilient markets need liquidity for next growth phase: RBI
articles.description: RBI Governor Sanjay Malhotra said India's resilient financial markets need deeper liquidity, broader participation, and stronger infrastructure for the next growth phase, citing the nation's strong macro fundamentals and prudent policy management.

articles.description: Step-Up SIP means you increase your monthly investment slightly each year, usually by 5 to 10%.

articles.title: MSRDC eyes private land for green scheme; draws Sena (UBT) ire
articles.description: MSRDC had floated an expression of interest to purchase private land across Maharashtra for compensatory afforestation earlier this week. An MSRDC official said that the land acquisition will be done “on direct purchase basis”

articles.title: SGNP puts steep pass hike on hold after backlash over ₹383- ₹10,000 jump
articles.description: Regular users said the increase disproportionately targeted walkers without addressing other facilities such as parking, the mini train and boating. Vilas Satpalakar, a daily visitor, questioned the rationale behind revising annual and monthly passes for morning walkers while leaving other charges untouched

articles.title: Centre plans ₹8,097 crore equity infusion for RINL revival in Visakhapatnam
articles.description: The government plans to announce an ₹8,097 crore financial package for RINL to aid its revival amid ongoing financial struggles.

articles.title: Staff crunch, infrastructure hit NCLT operations, must be addressed: Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court has taken suo motu action, highlighting severe infrastructure and vacancy issues plaguing the National Company Law Tribunals (NCLT). These deficiencies are causing significant delays in crucial insolvency and bankruptcy cases, potentially undermining the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code's objectives. The court emphasized the urgent need for immediate remedial measures to prevent further frustration of the IBC's purpose.

articles.title: "Don't work for...": Dianna Russini’s crisis deepens with reports of PR firms unwilling to manage Mike Vrabel-related controversy
articles.description: The controversy involving Dianna Russini and Mike Vrabel has deepened, with reports revealing struggles to secure effective PR support. CAA’s outreach reportedly met resistance, highlighting the reputational risks involved. As prior statements lost credibility and more images surfaced, professional consequences followed, including Russini’s resignation from The Athletic. Public criticism, notably from Emmanuel Acho, has intensified scrutiny, shifting the narrative from the incident itself to long-term credibility and career impact.

articles.title: Capricorn Horoscope Today for May 2, 2026: Love, children or hobbies may give relief from work pressure
articles.description: Career Horoscope Today: Money feels better when it gives comfort without regret.

articles.title: Political instability prevails in Punjab, says Haryana CM
